releases.title: collectZ v3.16.1
releases.summary: This patch finishes the visible Platform vs Workspace boundary cleanup by moving Merge Review out of the Platform nav group.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Changed Workspace into an expandable sidebar group when workspace-owned tools are available.
Moved `Merge Review` under Workspace.
Kept Platform focused on platform-level settings, runtime, activity, workspaces, and members.
Preserved the existing internal merge-review route ID and behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.0
releases.summary: This release clarifies the app boundary between workspace-owned collection work and platform-level setup.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Renamed the visible `Admin` nav group to `Platform`.
Renamed platform settings to `Platform Settings` with copy that explains the instance-wide scope.
Renamed workspace settings to `Workspace Settings` with copy that explains the selected-workspace scope.
Renamed workspace provider setup to `Workspace Integrations`.
Changed the hosted platform runtime surface to show platform runtime concerns instead of editable data-provider setup.
Dashboard provider health now points toward Workspace, where provider/data-sync setup belongs.

releases.title: collectZ v3.15.4
releases.summary: This patch adds a non-destructive restore rehearsal readback so admins can tell whether they have the pieces needed to practice a restore in a separate test stack.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `restore_rehearsal` to `GET /api/admin/settings/portability`.
Admin Settings now shows a Restore rehearsal card with database export, backup freshness, uploaded image binary, and restore dry-run checks.
The restore rehearsal response explicitly reports `destructive: false`.
Restore guidance now calls out rehearsal in a separate test stack.
The backup runbook now includes a restore rehearsal checklist.

releases.title: collectZ v3.15.3
releases.summary: This patch lets the backup and portability surface show whether an external scheduled backup job has reported a recent successful run.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added optional backup freshness readback to `GET /api/admin/settings/portability`.
Added a marker-file contract using `COLLECTZ_BACKUP_STATUS_PATH` and `COLLECTZ_BACKUP_FRESHNESS_HOURS`.
Admin Settings now shows a compact Backup freshness card next to database, images, and provider metadata.
Backup checks now include a freshness row with plain status detail.
The backup runbook documents the scheduled-backup marker JSON shape.

releases.title: collectZ v3.15.2
releases.summary: This patch adjusts backup export downloads so browsers receive plain JSON files and individual CSV files instead of archive wrappers.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: JSON backup exports now download as a single plain `.json` file.
CSV exports now expose a list of downloadable files instead of one zipped archive.
Admin Settings shows separate CSV download buttons for the manifest, restore guidance, uploads manifest, and each exported table.
The export API can return the CSV file list or one selected CSV file by key.
OpenAPI and browser regression coverage now document and verify the plain JSON and per-file CSV behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.15.1
releases.summary: This patch adds an explicit export format choice to the backup and portability workflow.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Admin Settings now lets admins choose between `JSON archive` and `CSV archive` before downloading an export.
`POST /api/admin/settings/portability/export` accepts `format: "json"` or `format: "csv"`.
JSON remains the default `.json.gz` portability archive.
CSV downloads a `.csv.zip` archive with manifest, restore guidance, uploads manifest, and one CSV per exported table.
JSON and CSV export paths share the same backend redaction behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.15.0
releases.summary: This minor release turns the backup and portability foundation into a real admin export action.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added an admin-only one-click export download in Admin Settings.
Added `POST /api/admin/settings/portability/export`.
Export downloads are gzip-compressed collectZ JSON bundles.
Export bundles include a manifest, app version, generation timestamp, portable database tables, upload-storage manifest, restore guidance, and redaction metadata.
Secret-looking fields and secret-looking URL parameters are recursively redacted before the export is written.
The existing portability readback now reports manual export capability.
Admin Settings shows the last successful export download for the current browser session.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.5
releases.summary: This patch adds practical edition and variant editing to Library drawers using the reusable collectible trait contract.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a compact Edition drawer section for Library records.
Edition controls now change by media type instead of using one generic variant field.
Books support edition, printing, publisher line, ARC, first edition, limited release, and numbered-run details.
Comics support variant cover, printing, run context, newsstand/direct edition, limited release, and numbered-run details.
Games support platform, region, release line, collector edition, limited release, and promo/demo state.
Movies, TV, and Audio support package, release, pressing, screener/promo, and limited-release style details.
Saved edition details appear through existing collectible trait readback and compact trait pills.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.4
releases.summary: This patch adds manual relationship workflows for linking related records without duplicating them across libraries.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a scoped `object_relationships` persistence table and migration.
Added authenticated relationship APIs for searching link targets, listing relationships for a record, saving links, and unlinking archived relationships.
Added a compact `Related` drawer section for Library, Art, and Collectibles records.
Relationship types include includes, part of, included with, companion to, purchased with, and event acquired with.
Relationship readback shows the related record title, record family, optional subtitle context, and optional note from both sides.

releases.title: collectZ v3.14.3
releases.summary: This patch adds owner-level proof and provenance editing for collectible-like records.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a compact Proof editor to Library, Art, and Collectibles detail drawers.
Proof details are stored through the reusable trait API as the `provenance` family.
Supported fields include proof type, issuer/source, certificate number, acquired-from/source context, evidence date, reference, and notes.
Saved proof details appear through existing collectible trait readback and trait pills.
Reference readback shows that a reference exists without echoing raw local path-style values in the drawer.
